Notice Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Oct-12-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: David Choi, for plaintiff and moving party: Gregory Daniels
Linda A. Ritchie, for defendant and opposing party: Samuel Brewster

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Gregory Daniels' motion to compel production of documents is granted. The objections, having been waived by the untimely response, are ordered stricken. The authority cited by defendant as to the issue of waiver is inapplicable as it does not pertain to CCP Section 2031 et seq. Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents within 10 days of notice of this order subject to the following: a) the parties are prohibited from using or disclosing the breath alcohol report for any purpose unrelated to the present action; and b) Counsel for plaintiff shall return the breath alcohol report, including all copies, at the conclusion of the litigation. The Court finds that defendant failed to respond in a timely fashion and asserted unmeritorious objections. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $540 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
